_doc: Language substrate registry for the reusable source-grounding pipeline. A source language is groundable iff it has an entry here. Adding a language = add an entry + download its Wiktionary extract (spaCy model is pip-installed by the ground stage). Only 'it' is verified; the rest are documented stubs to make the next book a config change, and MUST be verified before use (spaCy model quality and Wiktionary coverage vary by language).

it.notes: spaCy handles clitics/elisions (dell'->di il, Nell'->in il), gerunds (leggendo->leggere) and finite verbs that a dictionary lookup cannot. Verified 2026-07-11 on Calvino: it_core_news_sm (13MB) is IDENTICAL to it_core_news_lg (568MB) on lemma/POS/morph here (lg's word vectors are unused for grounding), so sm is the choice. Residual misses are isolated title words lacking sentence context (e.g. reflexive gerund 'Sporgendosi'). Wiktionary JSONL is keyed by lemma -> senses[] (gloss layer, Stage 2b, not yet built).

fr.notes: STUB — verify spaCy lemmatization + Wiktionary coverage before first use.

de.notes: STUB — German compounds/separable verbs need spot-checking before first use.

ru.notes: STUB — Cyrillic + rich inflection; verify before first use.
